title: GPS
puzzle: How can your phone find you by listening to satellites?
insight: GPS compares timing signals from multiple satellites. Because radio waves travel at a known speed, tiny timing differences become a location estimate.
key_ideas: Each satellite sends time and position information.
Travel time becomes an estimate of distance.
Several distances together reveal a position.
mind_blower: Navigation works because your device can measure incredibly small differences in time.
next_curiosity: If timing matters so much, what happens when those clocks drift?
narration_text: How can your phone find you by listening to satellites? GPS compares timing signals from multiple satellites. Because radio waves travel at a known speed, tiny timing differences become a location estimate. Each satellite sends time and position information. Travel time becomes an estimate of distance. Several distances together reveal a position. Navigation works because your device can measure incredibly small differences in time. If timing matters so much, what happens when those clocks drift?
